ip_outcome_note: Card name matches species name exactly. Depicted figure is Castform in Normal Form: small round white-lavender body, floating in cloudy sky. Vision pass already confirmed the form by color (white/lavender, not blue/Rainy, not orange/Sunny, not icy-white/Snowy). API confirms nationalPokedexNumbers: [351]. No ambiguity; Castform Normal Form verified.

trivia_bullets.text: Castform (National Pokedex no. 351) was introduced in Generation III (Ruby and Sapphire, 2002) as the first Pokemon to change type based on weather. Its four forms — Normal (no weather), Sunny (harsh sunlight, Fire-type), Rainy (rain, Water-type), and Snowy (hail/snow, Ice-type) — change via its Forecast Ability. The design is partly inspired by the Japanese teru teru bozu tradition: small cloth dolls hung to ward off rain. [Bulbapedia: Castform, via WebSearch]

In Castform's debut games, Pokemon Ruby, Sapphire, and Emerald, it was gifted to the player by a scientist at the Weather Institute as a reward for rescuing the facility from Team Aqua or Team Magma — both of whom sought to control weather on a global scale. Castform's origin as a 'weather researcher's companion' is baked into the game's lore. [Bulbapedia: Castform, via WebSearch]
"Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform's disposition!" `[PokemonTCG.io API: swsh9-116 flavorText]`

The Brilliant Stars Normal Form (BRS no. 116/172) is the fourth Castform form card in the SWSH era: the three alternate forms (Sunny, Rainy, Snowy) all appeared in Chilling Reign (CRE nos. 022, 033, 034/198). The Normal Form arriving in Brilliant Stars completes the full four-form set across SWSH-era printings. A Castform Rainy Form (CRE no. 033/198) is already in the corpus. [Limitless TCG: CRE/33, via WebSearch] [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh9-116]
